[{"review_id": "reviewid_135", "user_id": "userid_548", "business_ref": "businessref_34", "rating": "2", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.", "date": "August 01, 2016 at 03:44 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1067", "user_id": "userid_213", "business_ref": "businessref_89", "rating": "5", "useful": "2", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too", "date": "June 14, 2021 at 11:39 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_871", "user_id": "userid_616", "business_ref": "businessref_82", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this \"burger\" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our \"server\" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!", "date": "29 May 2013, 23:01"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_314", "user_id": "userid_1903", "business_ref": "businessref_66", "rating": "2", "useful": "1", "funny": "2", "cool": "1", "text": "This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare", "date": "21 May 2016, 18:48"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_487", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_95", "rating": "1", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened", "date": "November 01, 2021 at 05:11 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1031", "user_id": "userid_206", "business_ref": "businessref_24", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.", "date": "2013-07-08 21:47:00"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_441", "user_id": "userid_380", "business_ref": "businessref_40", "rating": "4", "useful": "0", "funny": "1", "cool": "0", "text": "You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.", "date": "March 02, 2009 at 09:43 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_40", "user_id": "None", "business_ref": "businessref_47", "rating": "5", "useful": "1", "funny": "0", "cool": "1", "text": "Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.", "date": "March 16, 2014 at 12:40 AM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1807", "user_id": "userid_1546", "business_ref": "businessref_16", "rating": "3", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.", "date": "September 18, 2014 at 05:50 PM"}, {"review_id": "reviewid_1741", "user_id": "userid_1348", "business_ref": "businessref_96", "rating": "5", "useful": "0", "funny": "0", "cool": "0", "text": "Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.", "date": "17 Apr 2017, 14:04"}]

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load the query results
wifi_businesses = var_functions.query_db_12

print(f"Total WiFi businesses found: {len(wifi_businesses)}")

# Count states
state_counts = {}
businesses_by_state = {}
wifi_values = {}

for business in wifi_businesses:
    wifi_value = business.get('attributes', {}).get('WiFi')
    wifi_values[str(wifi_value)] = wifi_values.get(str(wifi_value), 0) + 1
    
    description = business.get('description', '')
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2})[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_counts[state] = state_counts.get(state, 0) + 1
        if state not in businesses_by_state:
            businesses_by_state[state] = []
        businesses_by_state[state].append(business['business_id'])

print("WiFi values:", wifi_values)
print("State counts:", state_counts)
print("Businesses by state:", businesses_by_state)

# Get business IDs for Florida
fl_business_ids = businesses_by_state.get('FL', [])
print("Florida business IDs:", fl_business_ids)

# For debugging, let's check if we can get state counts for all businesses
all_wifi_businesses = var_functions.query_db_12
print(f"\nTotal businesses in result: {len(all_wifi_businesses)}")

# Print full details
for i, business in enumerate(all_wifi_businesses):
    print(f"\n{i+1}. {business['name']}")
    print(f"   ID: {business['business_id']}")
    print(f"   Description: {business['description']}")
    print(f"   WiFi: {business.get('attributes', {}).get('WiFi')}")

result = json.dumps({
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses),
    'state_counts': state_counts,
    'florida_business_ids': fl_business_ids
}, indent=2)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load the query results
wifi_businesses = var_functions.query_db_12

print(f"Total WiFi businesses found: {len(wifi_businesses)}")

# Count states
state_counts = {}
businesses_by_state = {}
wifi_values = {}

for business in wifi_businesses:
    wifi_value = business.get('attributes', {}).get('WiFi')
    wifi_values[str(wifi_value)] = wifi_values.get(str(wifi_value), 0) + 1
    
    description = business.get('description', '')
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2})[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_counts[state] = state_counts.get(state, 0) + 1
        if state not in businesses_by_state:
            businesses_by_state[state] = []
        businesses_by_state[state].append(business['business_id'])

print("WiFi values:", wifi_values)
print("State counts:", state_counts)
print("Businesses by state:", businesses_by_state)

# Get business IDs for Florida
fl_business_ids = businesses_by_state.get('FL', [])
print("Florida business IDs:", fl_business_ids)

# For debugging, let's check if we can get state counts for all businesses
all_wifi_businesses = var_functions.query_db_12
print(f"\nTotal businesses in result: {len(all_wifi_businesses)}")

# Print full details
for i, business in enumerate(all_wifi_businesses):
    print(f"\n{i+1}. {business['name']}")
    print(f"   ID: {business['business_id']}")
    print(f"   Description: {business['description']}")
    print(f"   WiFi: {business.get('attributes', {}).get('WiFi')}")

result = json.dumps({
    'total_wifi_businesses': len(wifi_businesses),
    'state_counts': state_counts,
    'florida_business_ids': fl_business_ids
}, indent=2)

print('__RESULT__:')
print(result)

args.code:
import json
import re

# Load the query results - using the variable name based on the tool call ID
wifi_businesses = var_functions.query_db_12

# Count states
state_counts = {}
businesses_by_state = {}

for business in wifi_businesses:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    # Look for pattern like "in City, State," to extract state
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2})[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_counts[state] = state_counts.get(state, 0) + 1
        if state not in businesses_by_state:
            businesses_by_state[state] = []
        businesses_by_state[state].append(business['business_id'])

# Get Florida business IDs
fl_business_ids = businesses_by_state.get('FL', [])

# Calculate average rating for Florida businesses
import var_functions.query_db_18 as fl_ratings_data

# Extract ratings for Florida businesses
fl_ratings = []
for row in fl_ratings_data:
    business_ref = row['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    if business_id in fl_business_ids:
        fl_ratings.append(int(row['rating']))

# Calculate average rating
avg_rating = sum(fl_ratings) / len(fl_ratings) if fl_ratings else None

# Prepare result
result_data = {
    'state_with_most_wifi': 'FL',
    'florida_wifi_business_count': state_counts.get('FL', 0),
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2) if avg_rating is not None else None,
    'total_fl_ratings': len(fl_ratings)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result_data))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

val_args.code:
import json
import re

# Load the query results - using the variable name based on the tool call ID
wifi_businesses = var_functions.query_db_12

# Count states
state_counts = {}
businesses_by_state = {}

for business in wifi_businesses:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    # Look for pattern like "in City, State," to extract state
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2})[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_counts[state] = state_counts.get(state, 0) + 1
        if state not in businesses_by_state:
            businesses_by_state[state] = []
        businesses_by_state[state].append(business['business_id'])

# Get Florida business IDs
fl_business_ids = businesses_by_state.get('FL', [])

# Calculate average rating for Florida businesses
import var_functions.query_db_18 as fl_ratings_data

# Extract ratings for Florida businesses
fl_ratings = []
for row in fl_ratings_data:
    business_ref = row['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    if business_id in fl_business_ids:
        fl_ratings.append(int(row['rating']))

# Calculate average rating
avg_rating = sum(fl_ratings) / len(fl_ratings) if fl_ratings else None

# Prepare result
result_data = {
    'state_with_most_wifi': 'FL',
    'florida_wifi_business_count': state_counts.get('FL', 0),
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2) if avg_rating is not None else None,
    'total_fl_ratings': len(fl_ratings)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result_data))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json

# Load WiFi businesses data
wifi_businesses = var_functions.query_db_12

# Count businesses by state
from collections import Counter
import re

state_counts = Counter()
businesses_by_state = {}

for business in wifi_businesses:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    # Extract state from description (pattern: "in City, State,")
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2})[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_counts[state] += 1
        if state not in businesses_by_state:
            businesses_by_state[state] = []
        businesses_by_state[state].append(business['business_id'])

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
most_common_state = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
print(f"State with most WiFi businesses: {most_common_state[0]} ({most_common_state[1]} businesses)")

# Get Florida business IDs
fl_business_ids = businesses_by_state.get('FL', [])
print(f"Florida business IDs: {fl_business_ids}")

# Load ratings data
ratings_data = var_functions.query_db_18

# Extract ratings for Florida businesses
fl_ratings = []
for row in ratings_data:
    business_ref = row['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    if business_id in fl_business_ids:
        fl_ratings.append(int(row['rating']))

print(f"Florida ratings: {fl_ratings}")
print(f"Number of ratings: {len(fl_ratings)}")

# Calculate average rating
if fl_ratings:
    avg_rating = sum(fl_ratings) / len(fl_ratings)
else:
    avg_rating = None

print(f"Average rating: {avg_rating}")

result = {
    'state': most_common_state[0],
    'wifi_business_count': most_common_state[1],
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2) if avg_rating is not None else None,
    'total_ratings': len(fl_ratings)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

import json

# Load WiFi businesses data
wifi_businesses = var_functions.query_db_12

# Count businesses by state
from collections import Counter
import re

state_counts = Counter()
businesses_by_state = {}

for business in wifi_businesses:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    # Extract state from description (pattern: "in City, State,")
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2})[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_counts[state] += 1
        if state not in businesses_by_state:
            businesses_by_state[state] = []
        businesses_by_state[state].append(business['business_id'])

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
most_common_state = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
print(f"State with most WiFi businesses: {most_common_state[0]} ({most_common_state[1]} businesses)")

# Get Florida business IDs
fl_business_ids = businesses_by_state.get('FL', [])
print(f"Florida business IDs: {fl_business_ids}")

# Load ratings data
ratings_data = var_functions.query_db_18

# Extract ratings for Florida businesses
fl_ratings = []
for row in ratings_data:
    business_ref = row['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    if business_id in fl_business_ids:
        fl_ratings.append(int(row['rating']))

print(f"Florida ratings: {fl_ratings}")
print(f"Number of ratings: {len(fl_ratings)}")

# Calculate average rating
if fl_ratings:
    avg_rating = sum(fl_ratings) / len(fl_ratings)
else:
    avg_rating = None

print(f"Average rating: {avg_rating}")

result = {
    'state': most_common_state[0],
    'wifi_business_count': most_common_state[1],
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2) if avg_rating is not None else None,
    'total_ratings': len(fl_ratings)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load WiFi businesses data from query_db:12
wifi_businesses = var_query_db_12

# Count businesses by state
state_counts = Counter()
businesses_by_state = {}

for business in wifi_businesses:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    # Extract state from description (pattern: "in City, State,")
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2})[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_counts[state] += 1
        if state not in businesses_by_state:
            businesses_by_state[state] = []
        businesses_by_state[state].append(business['business_id'])

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    most_common_state, max_count = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    
    # Get business IDs for the top state
    top_state_business_ids = businesses_by_state[most_common_state]
    
    # Load ratings data from query_db:18
    ratings_data = var_query_db_18
    
    # Extract ratings for businesses in the top state
    state_ratings = []
    for row in ratings_data:
        business_ref = row['business_ref']
        business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
        if business_id in top_state_business_ids:
            state_ratings.append(int(row['rating']))
    
    # Calculate average rating
    avg_rating = sum(state_ratings) / len(state_ratings) if state_ratings else None
    
    result = {
        'state': most_common_state,
        'wifi_business_count': max_count,
        'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2) if avg_rating is not None else None,
        'total_ratings': len(state_ratings)
    }
else:
    result = {'error': 'No WiFi businesses found'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

val_args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Load WiFi businesses data from query_db:12
wifi_businesses = var_query_db_12

# Count businesses by state
state_counts = Counter()
businesses_by_state = {}

for business in wifi_businesses:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    # Extract state from description (pattern: "in City, State,")
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2})[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_counts[state] += 1
        if state not in businesses_by_state:
            businesses_by_state[state] = []
        businesses_by_state[state].append(business['business_id'])

# Find state with highest number of WiFi businesses
if state_counts:
    most_common_state, max_count = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
    
    # Get business IDs for the top state
    top_state_business_ids = businesses_by_state[most_common_state]
    
    # Load ratings data from query_db:18
    ratings_data = var_query_db_18
    
    # Extract ratings for businesses in the top state
    state_ratings = []
    for row in ratings_data:
        business_ref = row['business_ref']
        business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
        if business_id in top_state_business_ids:
            state_ratings.append(int(row['rating']))
    
    # Calculate average rating
    avg_rating = sum(state_ratings) / len(state_ratings) if state_ratings else None
    
    result = {
        'state': most_common_state,
        'wifi_business_count': max_count,
        'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2) if avg_rating is not None else None,
        'total_ratings': len(state_ratings)
    }
else:
    result = {'error': 'No WiFi businesses found'}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

args.code:
import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Check what variables are available
print('Checking available variables...')
available_vars = [var for var in dir() if not var.startswith('_')]
print('Available variables:', available_vars)

# Try to access WiFi businesses data
wifi_data = None
ratings_data = None

for var_name in available_vars:
    if 'query_db_12' in var_name:
        wifi_data = globals()[var_name]
        print(f'Found WiFi data in {var_name}')
    elif 'query_db_18' in var_name:
        ratings_data = globals()[var_name]
        print(f'Found ratings data in {var_name}')

print('WiFi data type:', type(wifi_data))
print('Ratings data type:', type(ratings_data))

if wifi_data:
    print('WiFi data length:', len(wifi_data))
if ratings_data:
    print('Ratings data length:', len(ratings_data))

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps({'status': 'debug', 'wifi_len': len(wifi_data) if wifi_data else 0, 'ratings_len': len(ratings_data) if ratings_data else 0}))

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

var_functions.query_db:6.text: Sure, it's cheap, but there isn't much to see. I think you'd have to have a big interest in the topic to find it exciting, and kids would be bored. I think it only lasted maybe 10-15 minutes. Our tour person was somewhat knowledgeable and seemed into it, but he didn't give us much time to read the information on each level. I had to take photos of the plaques to read later, and half of them need replacing, as they are so sun-bleached they're virtually unreadable (tour guide said they're being replaced soon and that the other half were already replaced). I really thought it needed to be higher up to give a good view. The Lewis and Clark State Historic Site just down the road was more interesting and free. If you live around here like I do and have nothing better to do, you might want to give it a go if the topic interests you, but if you're a tourist, this is not something you should waste your time on.
Very good service but a little pricey for the services your receive. Clean and sanitary too

My friend and I enjoyed a fantastic meal at Miles Table and I can't wait to return! Given that it was half-price-burger-day, I felt the need to give the falafel burger a shot. It was delicious! I'm not always a falafel fan, but this "burger" was awesome. The brioche bun was the perfect consistency and the portion of fries was plentiful. My friend enjoyed the cobb salad (though she requested balsamic dressing instead of buttermilk and she received buttermilk). The restaurant is quaint and has ample seating. While you order at the counter, our "server" (I believe her name was Sarah) was fantastic and made sure we had everything we needed. Overall, this is a great spot and I will be back soon...I may have to make it a weekly lunch staple!

This location is not one of my favorites people here get pretty rude sometimes no one looks happy it's hit or miss with the food sometimes it's awesome other times terrible I always get honey walnut shrimp for an extra 1.25 which is retarded and Beijing beef I enjoy the rice n the noodles but idk do if u dare
Terrible service. I was charged twice for online order and they refused to refund me. Numerous times this has happened

A cornucupia of flavors and toppings. The area inside is kept clean and tidy. I had a vanilla with oreo. That was heavenly. The space can get crowded in there, so just be aware of that. It's all self-serve, and they let you have samples. Good business.

You can go to Artesano and enjoy the massive metal sculptures made exclusively for this gallery by a single artist in South America, and you can enjoy the large hanging art as well.  Rehabbers will appreciate the meticulous dedo of this old corner on Cresson.  But, for me whose foray into the purchase of original art extends to 3 encounters with minor minor artists, the attraction to Artesano is as a venue for small weddings and all sorts of parties for up to 120 guests.  The two level affair is beautiful, melding old original elements, weathered redone beams and brick with modern touches for wonderful result.  The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.

import json
import re
from collections import Counter

# Access the stored data directly by the variable names shown in storage
wifi_businesses = [
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2b2", "business_id": "businessid_64", "name": "Nail Care Salon", "description": "Located at 12337 Olive Blvd in Creve Coeur, MO, this establishment offers a range of services in Nail Salons, Beauty & Spas."},
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2b7", "business_id": "businessid_54", "name": "7-Eleven", "description": "Located at 13151 Race Track Rd in Tampa, FL, this establishment offers a variety of services including Service Stations, Coffee & Tea, Gas Stations, Automotive, Gas Stations, Food, and Convenience Stores."},
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2ba", "business_id": "businessid_91", "name": "Cafe Porche and snowbar", "description": "Located at 1625 Baronne St in New Orleans, LA, this vibrant establishment offers a delightful array of options ranging from Food, Shaved Ice, Cajun/Creole, Breakfast & Brunch, Party & Event Planning, Comfort Food, Cafes, Restaurants, to Event Planning & Services, making it a perfect spot for any occasion."},
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2bc", "business_id": "businessid_93", "name": "Callahan's Corner", "description": "Located at 914 Edwardsville Rd in Troy, IL, this vibrant spot offers a diverse menu featuring American (New) cuisine, along with a lively atmosphere perfect for nightlife, bars, restaurants, and sports bars."},
  {"_id": "6859a000fe8b31cd7362e2c1", "business_id": "businessid_26", "name": "McDonald's", "description": "Located at 7003 Seminole Blvd in Seminole, FL, this establishment specializes in a variety of offerings, including Fast Food, Restaurants, Coffee & Tea, Food, and Burgers, making it a convenient stop for a quick meal or a refreshing beverage."}
]

# Count businesses by state
state_counts = Counter()
businesses_by_state = {}

for business in wifi_businesses:
    description = business.get('description', '')
    match = re.search(r'in [^,]+, ([A-Z]{2})[,\.]', description)
    if match:
        state = match.group(1)
        state_counts[state] += 1
        if state not in businesses_by_state:
            businesses_by_state[state] = []
        businesses_by_state[state].append(business['business_id'])

# Find state with most WiFi businesses
most_common_state, max_count = state_counts.most_common(1)[0]
print(f"State with most WiFi businesses: {most_common_state} ({max_count} businesses)")

# Get business IDs for the top state
state_business_ids = businesses_by_state[most_common_state]
print(f"Business IDs for {most_common_state}: {state_business_ids}")

# Hardcode ratings data from query_db_18
ratings_data = [
  {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "4"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "2"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "2"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "2"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "5"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "4"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "4"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "2"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "5"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "4"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "3"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "3"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "4"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "5"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "5"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "4"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_26", "rating": "1"}, {"business_ref": "businessref_54", "rating": "1"}
]

# Extract ratings for businesses in the top state
state_ratings = []
for row in ratings_data:
    business_ref = row['business_ref']
    business_id = business_ref.replace('businessref_', 'businessid_')
    if business_id in state_business_ids:
        state_ratings.append(int(row['rating']))

print(f"Ratings for {most_common_state}: {state_ratings}")
print(f"Number of ratings: {len(state_ratings)}")

# Calculate average rating
avg_rating = sum(state_ratings) / len(state_ratings) if state_ratings else None
print(f"Average rating: {avg_rating}")

result = {
    'state': most_common_state,
    'wifi_business_count': max_count,
    'average_rating': round(avg_rating, 2) if avg_rating is not None else None,
    'total_ratings': len(state_ratings)
}

print('__RESULT__:')
print(json.dumps(result))

The caterer wont be that happy as they will work out of cramped quarters on the lower level, but its not that bad.   I should say all types of parties but Bar/Bat Mitzvahs as the hands off policy is super strict even though the metal sculptures will do more harm to a thirteen year old than they can do to it, but the wall hangings and fit and finish are not that durable.

Went today for my birthday!First blowout and my hair looks hot! Makeup done as well and it also is fabulous! I didn't have to wait at all got right is and was done at the appropriate time! I will be back ...even for just a scalp massage.

Tuesday for $2 pint night is the night to come to the fox. Everything on tap counts if they have it, but they do tend to run out of some of the good stuff. Food is ok... Got the steak tacos the other night, don't expect many dressings like sour cream, guac, or a lot of flavor. They also don't give you much  salsa, but it's edible.

Pretty sure this was the best meal I've ever had in my life. And that's saying something b/c I've traveled a lot, and I take my food seriously! I'm not a big seafood fan, to be honest nothing on the menu sounded super great. So I went for it and dove straight in to the 7 course chef's tasting menu. Best decision I've ever made. Highly recommend this place for a splurge.
